The Project
Castelo — Setúbal, Portugal
The Opportunity
A licensed, land-owned project
in one of Portugal's fastest-growing coastal cities

Castelo is a 14-unit high-end residential development in the historic centre of Setúbal, 500 metres from the sea and 30 minutes from Lisbon. The project has received full planning approval, the land is owned debt-free, and construction financing is pre-arranged with the bank.

Global Nest is offering qualified investors an equity participation in the Castelo project via a Portuguese CAEP contract. You sign a CAEP directly with the SPV (Forcing Line, Lda.) — your own profit-sharing contract defining your capital, returns, and exit rights. No intermediary, no Portuguese incorporation required. Separately, Global Nest holds a co-development agreement with the same SPV, giving it oversight, veto rights, and information access to protect investor interests throughout the project.

The base case LP IRR of 25% assumes modest 5% annual appreciation — well below Setúbal's actual 8–12% annual growth over the past three years. Even in a flat market (zero appreciation), LP returns still reach 20% IRR. Full scenario detail is shown in the table below.

Investment Structure
How it works
Your investment vehicle
CAEP Contract — Direct with SPV
Contrato de Associação em Participação signed directly with Forcing Line, Lda. Your profit participation is pro-rata to your capital contribution. You are a direct counterparty to the project vehicle — not a sub-participant.
Global Nest's role
Co-Development Partner
Global Nest holds a separate co-development agreement with the SPV — granting information access, decision veto rights, and active project oversight to protect investor interests. Global Nest governs execution; your CAEP governs your returns.
Return mechanism
Structured Waterfall
1. Return of capital pro-rata (90% LP / 10% GP)
2. 8% simple preferred return to LP (annual, 2 years)
3. 2% GP priority return (annual, 2.25 years)
4. Residual profit: 90/10 split + 20% GP promote from LP share — until 20% LP IRR
5. Above 20% LP IRR: 90/10 split + 50% GP promote from LP share
In practice
You receive your capital back first, then an 8% annual return before the GP earns any profit. The GP's success fee increases only after your return exceeds 20% IRR.
Minimum investment
€50,000
Capital deployed during the equity phase of the project (pre-construction). Exact timing communicated to investors in advance.
Tax treatment (Portugal)
PT resident individual
28% final withholding tax on distributions. No further filing required. Simple, definitive.
Non-resident individual
DTT (Double Tax Treaty) rate applies (10–15% for most nationalities). Model 21-RFI required before distribution.
CAEP classified as dividends
Per Portuguese DTT protocols, CAEP distributions = dividends. Reduced treaty rates available.
Security & Protections
How your investment is protected
1
Land owned debt-free

The SPV owns the land and construction licence outright. The bank's loan covers construction costs only. There is no acquisition financing risk — the asset base exists today.

2
Power of Attorney → Share Pledge on demand

Each investor receives an irrevocable POA (procuração irrevogável, Art. 265(3) CC) to register a pledge over its CAEP position in the SPV on defined trigger events — including payment default, or unauthorised asset disposal. Zero cost upfront; 0.5% Imposto do Selo only if exercised.

3
Minimum sale price covenant

The GPs cannot sell any SPV unit below the price level required to satisfy all investor capital and return obligations. This prevents the developer from discounting unsold units at the expense of investor returns.

4
LP-first waterfall — 8% preferred return before any GP profit split

Investors (LPs) receive an 8% simple annual preferred return before any residual profit is split. Only after LPs are made whole on capital plus preferred does the GP participate in residual profits. The 20%/50% GP's success fee applies only to the LP's share of residual profit — not to the preferred return or returned capital.

5
Contract duration ≥12 months — withholding tax dispensed for PT companies

For investors investing through a Portuguese company, withholding tax is dispensed under Art. 97(1)(c) CIRC when contract duration exceeds 12 months. The SPV pays IRC once; you receive your distribution subject only to your personal/corporate tax rate.

6
Dual-contract structure with direct investor rights

Your CAEP is signed directly with the SPV — your terms, returns, and exit rights are yours. Global Nest's separate co-development contract ensures active oversight, veto rights, and incentive alignment throughout the project.

Important Notice: This document is a private placement memorandum intended solely for qualified investors. It does not constitute a public offering of securities. Past performance does not guarantee future results. IRR, ROE, and equity multiple figures presented are estimates based on current market data and reasonable assumptions; actual returns may vary. Individual investor returns will depend on the timing and amount of capital deployment — as each investor may enter at a different point in the project timeline, realised IRR and multiples may differ from the illustrative figures shown. All investment in real estate development carries risk including the possible loss of principal. Equity positions are illiquid — your CAEP contract cannot be freely traded and exit is dependent on project completion or a negotiated transfer by Global Nest as CAEP associate. This document has been prepared by Global Nest Portugal (Leca Horizons Unipessoal, Lda.) for information purposes and does not constitute financial, legal or tax advice. Prospective investors should consult their own advisors before making any investment decision. The SPV (Forcing Line, Lda.) and the CAEP contract are governed by Portuguese law (Código das Sociedades Comerciais and Código Comercial respectively). Tax treatment described reflects current Portuguese law and may change. The legal structure described in this document (dual-contract framework, CAEP terms, co-development agreement, POA/pledge mechanics) is subject to final review and approval by legal counsel. Specific contractual terms, protections, and trigger events may be adjusted based on lawyer's final veto. Investors will receive final legal documentation upon counsel sign-off. April 2026.
